articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas

Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that enhances GPU and NPU acceleration for TensorFlow applications. This update aims to improve efficiency and security within the TensorFlow ecosystem.
articles.why_it_matters: Sets a new standard for high-performance AI deployment.

articles.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
articles.snippet: Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly, interactive "technical gym session" led by Google Cloud experts to help developers and architects sharpen their cloud skills. Moving beyond passive slide decks, the program focuses on hands-on building, covering advanced topics like AI agents, Vertex AI, and developer productivity tools. Each hour-long session is designed to provide tangible results that participants can immediately deploy into their own workflows. It serves as a consistent, dedicated space for
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly interactive session aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. The program focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Provides developers with practical skills to implement immediately.

articles.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
articles.snippet: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced a suite of updates focused on streamlining the core coding workflow through high-velocity tools like Agent Mode with Auto Approve and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements, along with new features for precise context management and custom commands, aim to transform the AI from a general assistant into a highly tailored, seamless collaborator that adapts to your specific development style.
articles.ai_summary: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to enhance collaboration between developers and AI.

articles.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
articles.snippet: Google has introduced Finish Changes and Outlines for Gemini Code Assist in IntelliJ and VS Code to reduce developer friction and eliminate the need for long, manual prompting. Finish Changes acts as an AI pair programmer that completes code, implements pseudocode, and applies refactoring patterns by observing your current edits and context. Meanwhile, Outlines improves code comprehension by generating interactive, high-level English summaries interleaved directly within the source code to help
articles.ai_summary: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce manual coding efforts. These tools help developers by completing code and generating summaries.
articles.why_it_matters: Reduces coding friction, making development more efficient.

articles.snippet: Agentic AI dominated RSAC 2026 as vendors grappled with invisible AI usage, shadow tools, unsecured end‑user agents and a renewed focus on the endpoint.
articles.ai_summary: At RSAC 2026, discussions centered around the rise of invisible AI agents, highlighting challenges with unsecured end-user agents and the need for better endpoint security.
articles.why_it_matters: Sheds light on the growing importance of security in AI deployment.

articles.title: neuravec-llm 0.3.1
articles.snippet: Async Azure OpenAI toolkit — structured output, agent pipelines, vision, token tracking
articles.ai_summary: Neuravec has released version 0.3.1 of its Async Azure OpenAI toolkit, which includes features for structured output, agent pipelines, vision, and token tracking.
articles.why_it_matters: Offers developers enhanced tools for building AI applications.

articles.title: Is Microsoft (MSFT) The Best AI Stock According to Billionaire Ken Fisher?
articles.snippet: We just covered Billionaire Ken Fisher’s Latest Portfolio: 10 Best AI Stocks to Buy. Microsoft Corp (NASDAQ:MSFT) ranks #3 (see the 5 Best AI Stocks to Buy)....
articles.ai_summary: Billionaire investor Ken Fisher has ranked Microsoft as one of the top AI stocks to buy, reflecting confidence in its growth potential in the AI sector.
articles.why_it_matters: Indicates strong investment interest in AI-driven companies.

articles.title: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
articles.snippet:
When Copilot cloud agent writes code, it automatically runs GitHub’s security and quality validation tools, including CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret scanning, and Copilot code review. If any problems…
The post Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
articles.ai_summary: GitHub's Copilot cloud agent has improved its validation tools, making them 20% faster for running security and quality checks on code.
articles.why_it_matters: Boosts efficiency in code validation, enhancing developer productivity.

articles.title: Britain seeks views before it drops the hammer on signal jammers
articles.snippet: Four-week call for evidence intended to help shape laws aimed at devices linked to crime The UK government is seeking views on radiofrequency jammers as it prepares legislation to ban the controversial devices.…
articles.ai_summary: The UK government is seeking public input on proposed legislation to ban radiofrequency jammers, which are often linked to criminal activities.
articles.why_it_matters: Addresses public safety concerns regarding the misuse of jamming devices.

articles.title: "Repeated choices to serve their business over their customers": Mozilla accuses Microsoft of using 'dark patterns' to force its Copilot AI on Windows 11 users
articles.snippet: Mozilla argues that Microsoft’s rollback of its aggressive Windows 11 Copilot campaign amounts to an admission that it relied on deceptive tactics and dark patterns to steer users into behaviors that serve its own interests.
articles.ai_summary: Mozilla has accused Microsoft of using deceptive practices to promote its Copilot AI in Windows 11, suggesting a rollback indicates reliance on dark patterns.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises ethical concerns about user manipulation in software design.

articles.title: Microsoft Removes Copilot Branding From Notepad in Windows 11, Replaces It With New “Writing Tools” Menu
articles.snippet:
Microsoft has started removing the Copilot branding from Notepad in Windows 11, with a new preview build now available to all Windows Insiders.
Thank you for being a Ghacks reader. The post Microsoft Removes Copilot Branding From Notepad in Windows 11, Replac…
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft has removed the Copilot branding from Notepad in Windows 11, replacing it with a new 'Writing Tools' menu in a recent preview build.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals a shift in Microsoft's approach to integrating AI features in its software.
